cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Jennifer could have gone to the swimming pool. So, the question is saying that today Jennifer went to the swimming pool, and we need to determine between which times she could have done that, given the various activities she was seen doing by different people. First, let me list out all the known activities and their times. Jennifer woke up at 5am. That's the starting point. Then, Richard saw her at the office from 5am to 8am. So, from 5 to 8, she was working at the office. Then, Samantha saw her reading at the library from 12pm to 1pm. Then Betty saw her buying a bike at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. Sarah saw her buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm. Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. Also, the swimming pool was closed after 10pm. The options given are A) 8am to 12pm, B) 9pm to 10pm, C) 2pm to 9pm, D) 12pm to 1pm. So, we need to see which of these time slots are not covered by her other activities, and also before the pool closed at 10pm. Let me break down her schedule: - 5am: Wakes up - 5am to 8am: At the office (with Richard) - Then from 8am to 12pm: What's happening here? The next activity mentioned is at 12pm to 1pm at the library. So between 8am and 12pm, there's no mention of her activities. So maybe she was free during that time? - 12pm to 1pm: Library with Samantha - 1pm to 2pm: Bike shop with Betty - 2pm to 9pm: Electronics store with Sarah buying a phone - 9pm to 10pm: Walking towards Statue of Liberty with Thomas - After 10pm: Pool is closed So, the possible free time would be between when she finishes one activity and starts another. Let's see: After finishing at the office at 8am, the next activity is at 12pm (noon) at the library. So from 8am to 12pm, there's no mention of her doing anything else. That's a four-hour window. So she could have gone to the pool during that time. Also, the pool is open until 10pm, so
